records.ety: [A comparative of forth; OE. further, forther, AS. fur<?/or, far<?/ur; akin to G. fürder. See Forth, adv.]

defs: To a greater distance; in addition; moreover. See Farther.
quotes: Carries us, I know not how much further, into familiar company.
They sdvanced us far as Eleusis and Thria; but no further.

defs: More remote; at a greater distance; more in advance; farther; as, the further end of the field. See Farther.
Beyond; additional; as, a further reason for this opinion; nothing further to suggest.
notes: ☞ The forms further and farther are in general not differentiated by writers, but further is preferred by many when application to quantity or degree is implied.

records.ety: [OE. furthren, forthren, AS. fyrðran, fyrðrian. See Further, adv.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Furthered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Furthering.]

defs: To help forward; to promote; to advance; to forward; to help or assist.
quotes: This binds thee, then, to further my design.
I should nothing further the weal public.
